title: Smoky Roasted Ethiopian Chicken Roast
description: A savory, tender chicken roast with a smoky rub featuring Szechuan peppercorns and Ethiopian spices-perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and set broiler on high.
Combine harissa, berbere, smoked paprika, cumin, Szechuan peppercorns, sea salt, black pepper, and olive oil in a small bowl to form a paste.
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels and rub the paste all over the exterior and cavity, then tuck lemon wedges inside.
Place the chicken on a roasting rack in a shallow pan and arrange lemon wedges on top.
Roast in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 165°F (74°C).
Remove from oven, tent loosely with foil, and let rest for 10 minutes; this allows juices to redistribute.
Increase oven temperature to 475°F (245°C) and switch to broiler mode.
Broil the chicken for 3–5 minutes per side, watching closely until the skin is crisp, slightly charred, and smoky—do not let it burn.
After broiling, rest the chicken for 5 minutes before carving.
Serve with a side of sautéed greens or cauliflower rice for a complete diabetic-friendly meal.
